subject: [PATCH v1 17/17] docs: fuse: document extended passthrough (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO)

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
The reviewer noted that the server must always set FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH if an inode is in passthrough mode, and it may also set FOPEN_DIRECT_IO to opt-out of passthrough when the inode is already in passthrough mode.

Reviewer noted that the documentation for extended passthrough mode is incomplete, specifically mentioning that iomodes were missing from the non-extended passthrough section.
reviews.raw_body:
IIUC (and please do double check this), the server MUST always set
``FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH`` if inode is in passthrough mode and it MAY set
``FOPEN_DIRECT_IO`` *additionally* to opt-out of passthrough when inode
is already in passthrough mode.

---

Looks nice.
Adds a few things (e.g. iomodes) that are missing in the doc
for the non-extended passthrough.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake in the documentation and agreed to correct it by changing the wording of FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH flag usage and adding a separate section for hybrid mode.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, you're correct. I'll change this to "must set
``FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH`` (and optionally ``FOPEN_DIRECT_IO``) on open as
cached..."

I'll add a separate section about the hybrid FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH |
FOPEN_DIRECT_IO mode to make it more clear that direct io supersedes
passthrough for read/write but not mmap.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-21.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) passthrough feature, specifically extended passthrough mode (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO). The new section explains how to enable and use this mode, which allows for one-to-one mapping of fuse inode numbers with backing file inode numbers. This enables passthrough for inode operations, directory operations, and kernel-initiated open requests.

2026-04-22.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) passthrough feature, specifically extended passthrough mode (FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO). The new section explains how to enable and use this mode, which allows for one-to-one mapping of fuse inode numbers with backing file inode numbers. This enables passthrough for inode operations, directory operations, and kernel-initiated open requests.
